nativeRange.habitat: Wetlands, marshes, and shallow water margins of streams and ponds across East Asia; grows both submersed and emersed in nature

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate or fine gravel preferred for anchoring stems
midground to background planting area, best in groups of stems
tolerates open swimming space above the planting

goodWith: fry that benefit from shelter in trailing foliage
avoidWith: plant-eating fish such as goldfish, silver dollars, or large cichlids
warm-water-only setups sustained above ~27 degrees C

summary: Japanese Cress (Cardamine lyrata) is an easy stem plant from East Asian wetlands, valued for its delicate, trailing foliage and unusual cold tolerance. It grows both submersed and emersed and produces aerial roots along its stems.
careNotes: Japanese Cress is a stem plant native to wetlands across Japan, China, and Korea, where it grows in the shallow margins of streams, ponds, and marshes. It is one of relatively few aquarium stem plants that is genuinely cold-tolerant, generally recommended for tanks kept below around 26–28 degrees C; sustained warmer temperatures tend to produce smaller leaves and leggier, less attractive growth. This cold tolerance also makes it suitable for unheated indoor tanks and for outdoor garden ponds during the warmer summer months. Substrate requirements are flexible — fine gravel or a nutrient-rich aquasoil both anchor the stems well, and cuttings root readily without special preparation. The plant grows in both submerged and emersed forms, and aerial roots tend to develop along the stems as it matures.
breedingNotes: Propagation is straightforward via stem cuttings: trim a healthy lateral shoot or top section and replant directly into the substrate, where it readily roots. Dense clumps can also be divided by splitting the rootstock. Aerial roots developing along the stems indicate good vigor and can themselves be used as propagation points.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source. Tropica describes Cardamine lyrata as an easy marsh plant from Japan with low light demand, medium CO2 (6-14 mg/L), fast growth, height 20-30+ cm, characteristic trailing stems with aerial roots, and suitability for unheated tanks and summer garden ponds. Note: the manifest's pre-supplied URL (4423/4423) actually serves Microsorum pteropus 'Windeløv'; corrected to 4441/4441 via Tropica's own search to reach the actual Cardamine lyrata page.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked common names (Japanese Cress, Chinese Ivy), family (Brassicaceae), Asian origin, pH 6.5-7.5, medium lighting, CO2 recommended, moderate-to-fast growth, midground placement, and stem-cutting propagation.
